Condition / branch: history-version is v1 or both
What integrity should do: Reject before importing core, scanning sources, or planning repairs.
How this avoids repeated work: Prevents unsupported branches from doing any work.

Condition / branch: --check-only and no --run-backfill
What integrity should do: Run the complete integrity scan and produce findings, but do not execute any repair.
How this avoids repeated work: Uses the same detection pass without starting a second workflow.

Condition / branch: --run-backfill requested
What integrity should do: Enable the v2 repair sequence for observations, manifests/indexes, and aqilevels.
How this avoids repeated work: One flag controls one ordered repair pass rather than separate manual repair runs.
Notes / decision to confirm: This should be restored for v2 only.

Condition / branch: Repair requested and environment is not an approved target
What integrity should do: Reject before invoking any writer.
How this avoids repeated work: Avoids partially running checks before discovering that writes are forbidden.
Notes / decision to confirm: Decide whether LIVE remains disabled or gets a separate explicit gate.

Condition / branch: Core root is missing or not history/v2/core
What integrity should do: Stop before source scans and report the configuration error.
How this avoids repeated work: Prevents later work using the wrong station/timeseries mappings.

Condition / branch: Repair mode requested but required wrapper, env, bucket, or credentials are missing
What integrity should do: Stop before scans or writers.
How this avoids repeated work: Avoids doing an expensive scan that cannot be followed by the requested repair.
Notes / decision to confirm: List the exact required settings for observ, manifest/index, and aqilevel repair.

Condition / branch: Latest snapshot hash is new
What integrity should do: Validate the v2 core manifest and import required core tables into the integrity database.
How this avoids repeated work: Imports once and reuses the result for all later stages.
Notes / decision to confirm: Required tables include connectors, stations, timeseries, phenomena, and mappings.

Condition / branch: Latest snapshot hash matches the last successful import
What integrity should do: Reuse the existing imported core tables.
On failure / blocked: Stop if existing import is invalid
How this avoids repeated work: Avoids repeatedly importing identical core parquet files.

Condition / branch: Manifest, key, hash, row count, or required table is invalid
What integrity should do: Do not scan or repair history.
How this avoids repeated work: Prevents all later work from being based on incomplete mappings.

Condition / branch: Source metadata and content are unchanged
What integrity should do: Record unchanged state and reuse stored source row/hour evidence.
On failure / blocked: Record adapter error if check failed
How this avoids repeated work: Avoids re-downloading and re-counting unchanged source files.

Condition / branch: File is new, changed, or reappeared
What integrity should do: Download, hash, cache, and derive source observation evidence.
On failure / blocked: Record failure and block affected scope
How this avoids repeated work: Processes each changed source file once, then shares its evidence across repair decisions.

Condition / branch: Expected source file is not available
What integrity should do: Record the missing source evidence and do not attempt an observation backfill for that scope.
How this avoids repeated work: Prevents repeated failed backfill attempts with no source data.

Task: Resolve source rows to v2 timeseries
Condition / branch: Source station/pollutant mapping exists
What integrity should do: Create or reuse source-to-timeseries row/hour evidence.
How this avoids repeated work: Resolves mappings once before any repair is selected.

Condition / branch: Source row cannot be mapped safely to a v2 timeseries
What integrity should do: Report operator action; do not run observ, manifest/index, or aqilevel repair for that affected scope.
How this avoids repeated work: Avoids repeatedly repairing data built from an uncertain mapping.

Condition / branch: Source and v2 observation evidence agree
What integrity should do: Mark observation data healthy.
How this avoids repeated work: Healthy scopes skip observation backfill completely.

Condition / branch: v2 parquet is missing, invalid, or has fewer valid source observations
What integrity should do: Add one scoped v2 observation repair candidate.
How this avoids repeated work: Combines all affected timeseries for the connector/day into one backfill call.
Notes / decision to confirm: Reconnect existing v2 observation repair path.

Condition / branch: v2 contains data not supported by current source evidence
What integrity should do: Report the discrepancy and require a safe explicit policy before deleting or rewriting data.
How this avoids repeated work: Avoids automatic destructive rewrites and repeated repair loops.
Notes / decision to confirm: No automatic DELETE should be inferred from source-only comparison.

Condition / branch: Parquet is valid but pollutant/connector/day manifest is missing, stale, or incomplete
What integrity should do: Add manifest/index repair actions without scheduling observation backfill.
How this avoids repeated work: Skips expensive observation rewriting when only hierarchy metadata is wrong.
Notes / decision to confirm: This is the new functionality being added to the original repair flow.

Condition / branch: Observation hierarchy is valid but index/latest/metadata is missing or stale
What integrity should do: Add only the required targeted index actions.
How this avoids repeated work: Avoids rebuilding child manifests that are already correct.

Condition / branch: Run is read-only
What integrity should do: Do not invoke observation, manifest/index, or aqilevel writers; finish the scan and report every planned action.
How this avoids repeated work: One detection pass supplies a complete preview without partial writes.

Condition / branch: All affected observation parquet is healthy
What integrity should do: Skip observation backfill and proceed directly to manifest/index repair.
How this avoids repeated work: Prevents unnecessary parquet rebuilds.

Condition / branch: Valid source evidence is available
What integrity should do: Run one scoped v2 observation backfill for the combined connector/day/timeseries set.
Writes history data?: Yes: v2 observ parquet and immediate child outputs
How this avoids repeated work: Batches all affected timeseries once before any parent manifest or AQI work.
Notes / decision to confirm: Confirm exact wrapper and whether it already writes pollutant manifests.

Condition / branch: Target parquet already matches the requested source data
What integrity should do: Treat as a successful no-op and continue to verification.
On failure / blocked: Mark failed only if verification disagrees
How this avoids repeated work: Avoids rerunning the writer just because a repair was planned from stale evidence.

Condition / branch: Writer exits non-zero or output cannot be verified
What integrity should do: Block manifest/index and aqilevel repair for that scope.
How this avoids repeated work: Prevents downstream work from being built on uncertain observation data.

Condition / branch: Re-read repaired parquet and compare against source evidence
What integrity should do: Confirm required rows/timeseries/timestamps are now present and valid.
How this avoids repeated work: One verification replaces repeated speculative backfills.

Condition / branch: Recompute manifest/index requirements from the verified final observation state
What integrity should do: Discard stale pre-repair parent-manifest assumptions and build final actions from current children.
How this avoids repeated work: Ensures manifests are built once from final parquet rather than before and after backfill.

Condition / branch: Use the original verified manifest/index actions
What integrity should do: Pass the scoped actions directly to the Phase 3 executor.
How this avoids repeated work: Avoids rerunning observation checks before metadata-only repair.

Condition / branch: Rebuild the selected pollutant manifest from verified parquet.
What integrity should do: Phase 3 executor / specialist writer
On failure / blocked: Writes each child manifest once from final observation data.
How this avoids repeated work: Existing Phase 3 executor

Task: All pollutant children for connector are verified
Condition / branch: Build one connector manifest from the complete live/staged pollutant set.
What integrity should do: Phase 3 executor
On failure / blocked: One connector parent is produced after all child changes, not once per pollutant.
How this avoids repeated work: Existing Phase 3 executor

Task: All connector children for day are verified
Condition / branch: Build one day manifest from the complete live/staged connector set.
What integrity should do: Phase 3 executor
On failure / blocked: One day parent is produced after all connector work, avoiding repeated day rebuilds.
How this avoids repeated work: Existing Phase 3 executor

Task: Day hierarchy changed or index is independently stale/missing
Condition / branch: Rebuild only the targeted observation index/latest/metadata objects.
What integrity should do: Phase 3 index builder
On failure / blocked: Indexes are written once after the complete day hierarchy is final.
How this avoids repeated work: Existing Phase 3 executor

Task: Proposed object body matches existing object
Condition / branch: Skip the PUT and report unchanged.
What integrity should do: Phase 3 executor
On failure / blocked: Idempotence prevents repeated writes on later integrity runs.
How this avoids repeated work: Existing

Task: A manifest or index object was changed
Condition / branch: Freshly GET and verify the exact written body and hierarchy references.
What integrity should do: Phase 3 executor
On failure / blocked: A single verification gate prevents downstream AQI work on unverified metadata.
How this avoids repeated work: Existing

Condition / branch: Do not queue aqilevel repair.
What integrity should do: AQI planner
On failure / blocked: Prevents O3 and other unsupported pollutants from entering the AQI workflow.
How this avoids repeated work: Existing
Current implementation status: Eligible pollutants currently no2, pm25, pm10.

Condition / branch: Block aqilevel repair for the affected scope.
What integrity should do: Repair coordinator
On failure / blocked: Avoids building AQI from incomplete or unverified observations.
How this avoids repeated work: Existing logic partly present

Task: All expected supported-pollutant UTC hours already exist
Condition / branch: Mark aqilevels healthy and do not rebuild.
What integrity should do: v2 AQI hourly integrity check
On failure / blocked: Uses hour identities, so hundreds of raw rows do not trigger repeated AQI rebuilds.
How this avoids repeated work: Existing Phase 9 logic

Task: One or more expected UTC hours are missing, or AQI output/manifest is invalid
Condition / branch: Queue one scoped aqilevel rebuild.
What integrity should do: AQI repair planner
On failure / blocked: Deduplicates all missing hours into one connector/day AQI rebuild.
How this avoids repeated work: Existing but disconnected

Condition / branch: Run the v2 hourly AQI writer once for the connector/day.
What integrity should do: Existing AQI rebuild writer
Component / command: Yes: v2 aqilevel parquet/manifests/indexes as designed
On failure / blocked: One rebuild covers all affected eligible pollutants/hours for that scope.
How this avoids repeated work: Existing code, currently disabled
Current implementation status: Confirm exact writer scope and outputs.

Condition / branch: Re-read actual AQI hour identities and verify every expected hour now exists.
What integrity should do: v2 AQI post-rebuild integrity check
On failure / blocked: Prevents repeated AQI rebuilds when the writer claims success but output is incomplete.
How this avoids repeated work: Existing code/fixtures need alignment
Current implementation status: Use hour identities, not raw row counts.

Task: All planned repair stages have finished
Condition / branch: Run one final v2 integrity recheck over the affected date range.
What integrity should do: Integrity checker in verification mode
On failure / blocked: A single final pass replaces separate repeated checks after every task.
How this avoids repeated work: Existing post-repair logic, currently disconnected
Current implementation status: Can optimise by checking affected scopes while still producing a complete final report.

Condition / branch: Mark the run successful.
What integrity should do: Run status/reporting
On failure / blocked: The next scheduled run starts clean and performs no repairs.
How this avoids repeated work: Existing

Condition / branch: Mark run failed and report exact remaining observation scopes; do not automatically start a second repair loop.
What integrity should do: Run status/reporting
On failure / blocked: One repair pass per run prevents uncontrolled repeated backfills.
How this avoids repeated work: Needs confirmation
Current implementation status: A later run may retry after investigation.

Condition / branch: Mark run failed and report exact keys/actions; do not automatically repeat Phase 3 in the same run.
What integrity should do: Run status/reporting
On failure / blocked: Avoids loops caused by a persistent hierarchy or concurrency defect.
How this avoids repeated work: Needs confirmation

Condition / branch: Mark run failed and report missing expected UTC hours; do not automatically repeat the AQI writer.
What integrity should do: Run status/reporting
On failure / blocked: Prevents repeated AQI rewrites when the real cause is unresolved observation or writer logic.
How this avoids repeated work: Needs confirmation

Condition / branch: Run completed, failed, blocked, or was check-only
What integrity should do: Record findings, planned actions, executed actions, changed objects, verifications, blocked scopes, and final status.
How this avoids repeated work: Provides one authoritative record instead of reconstructing work from multiple commands.

Condition / branch: Scheduled run and reporting integration enabled
What integrity should do: Publish one final task status reflecting the complete ordered workflow.
How this avoids repeated work: Avoids reporting success before downstream manifest/AQI work finishes.
